title: CRISPR Therapeutics Posts $43M CASGEVY Revenue in Q1 2026
text: CRISPR Therapeutics Q1 2026 CASGEVY Commercial Update. Recent Sales Performance: CASGEVY generated first quarter 2026 revenue of $43 million, with more than 500 people globally having initiated the CASGEVY treatment journey, reflecting continued commercial momentum. This represents significant growth from approximately $14.2 million in the year-ago period. Full-Year 2025 Performance: CASGEVY delivered $116 million in 2025 revenue from 64 infused patients, with global approvals and reimbursement expanding across the U.S., Europe and the Middle East. Geographic Expansion: CASGEVY is approved in the U.S., Canada, the U.K., the EU, Switzerland, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, the Kingdom of Bahrain, Qatar, the United Arab Emirates, and Kuwait for patients 12 years and older with severe sickle cell disease or transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, with more than 60,000 eligible patients in these countries. Reimbursement Progress: As of year-end 2025, approximately 90% of patients in the U.S. had reimbursed access to CASGEVY, which is also reimbursed in the U.K., Italy, Austria, Denmark, Luxembourg, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, the UAE, and Kuwait. Future Outlook: Vertex has submitted a regulatory filing seeking label expansion for Casgevy in patients aged five to 11 years with sickle cell disease and transfusion-dependent beta thalassemia, with plans to use the Commissioner's National Priority Voucher to significantly cut down the review period to 1-2 months.
